observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to the following:

Identify the amounts and names of creditors and submit the schedules of all the liability accounts to support the balances of these accounts in the financial statements; and 

Undertake a thorough review and analysis of its details and based on the schedules, identify those without valid claimants and consider reversion of the accounts to the unappropriated surplus in accordance with Section 98 of PD No. 1445, where applicable.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to make extra efforts to reconcile the discrepancies noted, make the appropriate disclosures, and the necessary adjustments/ corrections in the books of accounts to reflect the correct balances of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to identify the amounts and names of debtors and submit the schedules and status of all the receivable accounts to support the balances in the financial statements.

We recommended and management agreed, thru the Municipal Accountant, use the actual amounts in the SCBAA including those under continuing appropriation, for the reconciliation between actual amounts on a comparable basis as presented in the statement and in the Statement of Financial Performance.

We recommended and management agreed to the following: 

The Municipal Accountant exert extra effort to identify the reconciling items noted in the BRS and record adjustments for valid reconciling items; and

Prepare the bank reconciliation statements for the bank accounts of the general fund in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011.

We recommended and management agreed to the following:

The Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records monthly to ensure transparency and accurate reporting of the SAAOB and SCBAA;

The Municipal Budget Officer to provide the updated RAAOs to the Municipal Accountant; and

Municipal Budget Officer to furnish copies of the approved, budget, supplemental budgets, realignment and distribution of the lump sum appropriation and the corresponding legislative approval to the Municipal Accountant.

We recommended and management agreed thru the Municipal Accountant to conduct analysis of the Accounts Payable and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify those improperly accounted financial transactions into its appropriate liability accounts.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to ensure that concerned officials prepare and submit the complete consolidated financial statements and schedules on or before February 14 of each year pursuant to the stated regulations to enable the auditor to verify, validate and audit the accounts thoroughly and promptly. Also, for the concerned offices to submit the monthly trial balance and SAAOB as required.

We recommended and management agreed to direct the:

Municipal Treasurer to promptly submit the DVs and supporting documents to the Office of the Municipal Accountant; and

Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate submission of all unsubmitted DVs and its supporting documents.

We recommended and management agreed to the following:

Establish clear and comprehensive policies and procedures outlining the process for requesting, approving, and liquidating cash advances. This includes specifying deadlines for advance liquidation and outlining consequences for non-compliance;

The Municipal Accountant to not authorize in their level the granting of cash advances to officers and employees with pending unliquidated cash advances; and

Implementing procedures for regular reconciliation and reporting of cash advances to track their status and ensure timely liquidation. This includes monitoring advance balances and following up on overdue balances.

We recommended and the LDRRM Officer agreed that in the preparation of the LDRRMFIP, they will include in a separate caption, the PPAs to be charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years or those which were transferred to the Special Trust Fund.

We recommended and management agreed to direct the:

1. Xxx

Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate submission of all unsubmitted DVs and its supporting documents.

We recommended and management agreed to the following: 

The MDC realigns the PAPs that were not implemented in CY 2022 totaling P1.586 million to other viable development projects, subject to the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan;

Engage in meaningful consultation and participation with relevant stakeholders to help ensure that the 20% DF programs is responsive to the needs of the community to avoid too many changes in the budget; and

The BAC, Municipal Engineer, MPDC and MDC properly plan and schedule all procurement activities pertaining to development projects to pave the way for their timely implementation and completion.
We recommended and the accountable officers agreed to prepare RAAF and CRAAF at the end of each month and submit the CRAAF to the audit team.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer deposit intact all her collections, as well as collections turned over to her by the liquidating officer with the authorized depository bank pursuant to Section 26 of COA Circular No. 92-382.

We recommended that the Accountant submits from now on the monthly pre-closing trial balance for each fund not later than 20th day after the end of the month, and the trial balance at the end of the quarter with supporting schedule of subsidiary ledger balances of the controlling accounts in the General Ledger.
The municipal accountant shall prepare and submit the monthly bank reconciliation statement within the prescribed period to facilitate verification and analysis of cash account.

Recommendation
We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
Prioritize the preparation and finalization of the CY 2024 SCBAA and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor as soon as possible; and
Moving forward, and in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer, conduct monthly or quarterly reconciliations of the accounting and budget records to facilitate the timely preparation and submission of the SCBAA, together with the other financial statements, not later than the 14th day of February after the end of each calendar year.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to:
Allocate funds for the transfer of title and registration of land in favor of the LGU. Henceforth, facilitate the registration and transfer of titles of land to ensure ownership and fully protect the interest of the LGU; and
Coordinate with appropriate national government agencies such as the Registry of Deeds and secure copies of the land titles of the parcels of land which allegedly have been titled in favor of the LGU.

Recommendation
We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
Prepare and submit supporting schedules of the affected liability accounts with complete details/breakdown such as, but not limited to, names of creditors, nature of transactions or particulars, reference numbers, transaction dates, and amounts;
Undertake a thorough review and analysis of accounts based on the supporting schedules, determine the validity of the accounts particularly those reported as accounts payable, and consider the reversion of long outstanding balances to the unappropriated surplus in accordance with Section 98 of PD No. 1445, as warranted; and
Based on available supporting schedules, consider the request for the write-off of applicable dormant liability accounts in accordance with the guidelines and procedures under COA Circular No. 2016-005.

Recommendation
We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to identify the discrepancies between the reciprocal accounts, Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, reconcile the same, and effect the necessary adjustments, as warranted, to reflect the correct account balances for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.
Henceforth, initiate the return or settlement of government funds from one fund to another, as appropriate, to come up with zero balances of the said accounts. Otherwise, the Municipal Accountant initiate the request for the write-off of the applicable account balances in accordance with the guidelines and procedures under COA Circular No. 2016-005.
Management Comment
The Municipal Accountant commented that they have already traced back prior years’ records and prepared the schedules/breakdowns of the Due from Other Funds account and would conduct verification and reconciliation of balances once they have finished the preparation of the schedules/breakdowns of the Due to Other Funds account.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management, through the Municipal Accountant, agreed to:
Exert efforts to trace back prior years’ records and determine the unidentified beginning/prior years’ balances of the affected receivable accounts. Otherwise, initiate the request for the write-off of applicable dormant receivable account balances in accordance with the guidelines and procedures under COA Circular No. 2016-005;
Coordinate with concerned barangay officials for the immediate submission of liquidation documents relative to the funds transferred by the LGU to the barangays, as a basis for the dropping of the Due from LGUs accounts; and
Make representations with the BIR relative to the refund of over-remittances of taxes. In case a refund is not granted nor viable, the concerned employee/official responsible for such over-remittances refund the subject amounts. Henceforth, properly monitor the withholding and remittance of taxes so as to avoid under/over-remittance of the same.

Recommendation
We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the necessary adjustments to record and recognize the cash in bank account balances maintained with LBP pertaining to depository account numbers 0522-1404-49 and 0522-1404-30 with bank balances of P4,993,065.09 and P10,000.00, respectively, as of December 31, 2024.

Recommendation
We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
Exert efforts to prepare the BRS of the cash in bank accounts under the General Fund with depository account numbers 0840-019263-080 maintained in DBP and 0522-1100-51 maintained in LBP;
Prepare the necessary adjustments to record the identified book reconciling items with a total net amount of P331,859.13; and
Exert efforts to identify the book reconciling items with a total net amount of P2,709,524.43 noted in the BRS of various cash in bank accounts and prepare the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts of the LGU.
We further recommended, and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to prepare, maintain, and update the cashbooks of all cash in bank accounts of the LGU. Henceforth, conduct periodic reconciliation with the records maintained by the Municipal Accounting Office.
Management Comment
The Municipal Accountant commented that they have already started the preparation of the BRS of the subject cash in bank accounts under the General Fund and committed to submit the same to the Office of the Auditor the soonest possible time.

Recommendation
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to carefully review financial transactions and record the same in accordance with the Revised Charts of Accounts as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-009.

Recommendation
We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to fully observe the Perpetual Inventory Method by initially recording the purchases of supplies and materials as inventories and subsequently recognizing the same as expenses upon issuance/distribution to end-users/intended beneficiaries, except for those purchased out of the petty cash fund, which shall be charged immediately to the appropriate expense accounts.

Recommendation
We recommended, and the Management agreed through the Municipal Accountant to present the effects of changes in accounting policy and prior period errors/adjustments by restating the prior year’s balances of the affected accounts shown as corresponding figures in the current year comparative FS in compliance with the requirements of IPSAS 3.

Recommendation
We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare and maintain the SPLCs to record the acquisition, description, custody, impairment, issue/transfer/disposal, repair history, and other information about the semi-expendable property.
Further, we recommended, and the GSO agreed to:
Prepare and maintain the SPCs to record the acquisition, issue/transfer/disposal and the description/information about the semi-expendable property;
Prepare and maintain ICSs and have the end-users of the semi-expendable properties affix their signatures to establish accountability over such assets;
Prepare and maintain the RegSPI to record the issue, return, reissue, disposal, and other information about the semi-expendable property if returned and its description/information; and
Attend seminars/trainings on Supply and Property Management and other related subject matters.

Recommendation
We recommend, and Management, through its LDRRM Council and LDRRM Officer, agreed to:
Ensure that the identified LDRRM PPAs are properly and timely implemented to achieve the desired objectives and outcomes of the LGU;
Fast track the completion and/or implementation of disaster risk reduction and management PPAs funded out of the continuing appropriations that remained unimplemented as of year-end. Otherwise, consider the reprogramming of the same, which may no longer be needed, into other priority disaster risk reduction and management PPAs; and
For fully completed/implemented PPAs, realign or reprogram the excess balances of continuing appropriations of the LDRRM Fund to finance other priority disaster risk reduction and management PPAs of the LGU.

Recommendation
We recommended, and the LDRRM Council, through the LDRRM Officer, agreed to include in the LDRRMFIP a separate caption containing the list of priority PPAs chargeable against the unexpended LDRRM Fund of previous years which were transferred to the STF, in addition to the list of PPAs chargeable against the current year’s appropriation for the five per cent LDRRM Fund in the General Fund.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to the following:
Through the Local Chief Executive, organize and establish the CCT who shall be responsible for the formulation, refinement, finalization, testing, implementation, evaluation, packaging, updating, and improvement of their organization’s PSCP;
Coordinate with the Office of Civil Defense (OCD) on the conduct of relevant trainings/seminars on the formulation of the PSCP; and
Through its CCT, finalize its PSCP in accordance with the PSCP Guidebook 2nd Edition, and submit the same to the DND-OCD through the Capacity Building and Training Service (CBTS) for consolidation and databasing.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to:
Furnish the Office of the Auditor at the beginning of every calendar year a list of all its ongoing PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year;
Prepare and submit a complete Quarterly Report on Government PPAs containing not only infrastructure projects but also non-infrastructure PPAs, if any; and
Inform the Office of the Auditor within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted.

Recommendation
We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare monthly trial balances and quarterly financial statements together with the required reports and supporting schedules such as the Status of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations, and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within the reglementary period.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management, through the GSO, agreed to:
Furnish the Office of the Auditor with copies of contracts/purchase orders related to the procurement of goods, services and infrastructure projects, including the relevant supporting documents, within five (5) days after execution to facilitate timely review and evaluation of the same; and
Submit notice of deliveries/completion to the Office of the Auditor related to procured goods/infrastructure projects within the prescribed period to facilitate the conduct of inspection.

Recommendation
We recommended that collecting officers observe the daily remittance of collections to the Municipal Treasurer to prevent the accumulation of funds in their possession and to prevent the loss and/or misuse of government funds.
Management Comment
The Municipal Treasurer commented that he has already informed and instructed all collecting officers to remit their cash collections daily to the Municipal Treasurer’s Office.

Recommendation
We recommended that the collecting officer/s and the Municipal Treasurer prepare the RAAF and Consolidated RAAFs, respectively, and observe the prescribed period for the submission of the same to the Office of the Auditor not later than the 5th day of the ensuing month.
Management Comment
The Municipal Treasurer commented that he has already informed and instructed all collecting officers to prepare and submit their respective RAAFs.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to correct the noted deficiencies and comply with the fire safety measures prescribed under Rule 10, Chapter 2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9514 to ensure the safety of the occupants of government buildings and structures and to prevent the risks of damage or loss of life and property.

We recommended and management agreed to reconcile the discrepancy, make the necessary corrections, and carefully review the FS and supporting schedules to ensure accuracy and compliance with applicable accounting standards and regulations.
We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustment to properly reflect the correct balance of the CY 2023 Loans Payable account.

We recommended and management agreed through the Municipal Budget Officer to:

Prepare the SAAOB in accordance with Annex E of COA Circular 2010-001 specifically in the presentation of the subject of appropriation by expenditure class: PS, MOOE, CO or FE; and

Prepare and submit SAAOB for continuing appropriations.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to refrain from acting as disbursing officer, instead, grant the cash advance to existing Disbursing Officers provided they have no unliquidated cash advances. In case of unavailability of existing Disbursing Officers, the Municipal Mayor may designate an LGU personnel to act as Special Disbursing Officer provided the requirements for fidelity bonding are complied with.

We recommended and the Municipal Engineer agreed to submit to the audit team the quarterly status report of its PPAs on before the 10th day after the end of the quarter for compliance and monitoring purposes.

We recommended and management agreed to the following:

The Municipal Budget Officer reconstruct the RAAOs of the 20 per cent DF continuing appropriations and present it on an annual basis. Also, separate the funds from other sources to report the portion of balances financed by 20 per cent DF; and

The Municipal Budget Officer coordinate with the Municipal Development Council through the MDC Secretariat for the purpose of providing the audit team with the necessary documentation to validate the realignment of funds.

We recommended and management agreed to:

Prepare and maintain complete journals, general ledgers and subsidiary ledgers to determine at any time the details and status of the accounts and to facilitate verification of the FS; and

Submit promptly in succeeding years the complete set of journals and ledgers in printed and electronic copies to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended and management agreed to the following: 

Conduct a thorough assessment of the condition of the building to determine the extent of damage and identify areas that need repairs;

Consider lowering the bond or possible repurposing the building to maximize its potential use for the benefit of the community; and

Adequate planning be made on projects to be implemented to avoid unwarranted spending of government resources.

We recommended and management agreed to require the submission of eligibility documents of the supplier in the procurement of fuel through small value procurement to be attached to the disbursement vouchers.
We recommended and management agreed to cover their JO personnel acting as sub-collectors with fidelity bond to protect the LGU against possible financial losses.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Determines the respective causes of the abnormal balances of the accounts and submit the findings with recommendations and propose adjustments to effect the necessary adjustments as may be warranted; and, xxx.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare for each LEE and PU of the LGU, the subsidiary ledger, trial balance and financial statements as required in COA Circular No. 2020-004.
For the unpaid penalty totaling P0.952 million, the LGU may make representations with the MDFO-DOF and ask for a waiver or negotiate with much lower amount

We recommended that the GFPS recommend to the SB the passing of the ordinance localizing RA 11331 and post the same in conspicuous places and include provisions on the following: measures to prevent gender-based sexual harassment in educational institutions; imposition of fines; and creation of sexual harassment hotlines.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer enforce the remedies and measures prescribed under Section 254 and 258 of RA 7160 to collect delinquent real property taxes. Strengthen collection system through intensified tax campaign to eliminate and/or minimize delinquencies.

We recommended and management agreed to direct the:

Municipal Treasurer to promptly submit the DVs and supporting documents to the Office of the Municipal Accountant and provide an explanation on the accumulation of DVs in his custody; and

Municipal Accountant cause the immediate submission of all unsubmitted DVs and their supporting documents and ensure complete supporting documents before certifying the DV for processing of payment.

We recommended that management use the prescribed form of Driver’s Trip Ticket in COA Circular No. 75-A and fill out the details as required.

We recommended and management agreed to comply with the posting requirements in the conspicuous place of the LGU and in the GPPB Online Portal within 30 days after the award of contract for purposes of accountability and transparency.

We recommended and management agreed to the following:

1. PNP operations be charged to confidential fund;

2. The Municipal accountant require proper documentation depending on the nature of the disbursement; and

3. Record faithfully the transactions using the account title that truly reflects the nature of the disbursement with reference to COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended and management agreed to follow the legal procedures in the reprogramming of PPAs and appropriation of funds in the LDRRMF by seeking the approval of the MDRRMC and the SB first before any disbursements are made.

We recommended and management agreed to carefully analyze the nature of the expenses of the programs in the LDRRMF and classified them accordingly; and

The balance under the program “Repair and Maintenance of DRRM Vehicles, Equipment and Purchase of Spare Parts and Tools Needed for Maintenance” be transferred to the trust fund.
We recommended that management reconstitute their LGU GAD Focal Point System to include the Chairperson on Committee on Appropriation (CCA) of the Sanggunian Bayan as member.

We recommended and management agreed to refrain from hiring a legal consultant, instead, consult its legal matters with the Provincial Legal Officer and strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 98-002 dated June 9, 1998; and

That the hiring of consultants must be through competitive bidding unless the existence of conditions warrant the application of any alternative methods of procurement.

We recommended and management agreed to capacitate its GFPS on how to undertake gender analysis by sending them to trainings/seminars and

require the creation and maintenance of GAD database containing sex-disaggregated data and other gender-related information which shall serve as basis in conducting gender analysis, identifying gender issues and determining the appropriate PAPs that would address the identified gender issues to be incorporated in the GAD Plan and Budget.

We recommended and management agreed that the Committee on Women and Family with the assistance of the GFPS revise and update the LGU GAD Code to include the legal mandate (i.e. RA 9710 and other policies implementing gender equality and empowerment) and 

submit the same to the sanggunian for enactment into Ordinance.

We recommended that management:

 Purchase its common-use supplies and equipment thru DBM-PS to avail of the most advantageous price. If the items to be procured are not available thereat, attached the CNAs and a BAC Resolution recommending and justifying to the HOPE the use of alternative mode of procurement and approval by the HOPE of the BAC Resolution recommending award of contract; and

Strict compliance regarding the conduct of bidding for acquisition of goods and services exceeding P200,000.00 (for 3rd class municipalities) and on the prohibition of splitting of purchase requisitions to avail of bulk discounts.

We recommended that:

The Municipal Mayor effect the full utilization of the LDRRMF in order to strengthen the Municipality’s disaster preparedness.

Make representations with the HDMF to consider the over remittance of Pag-IBIG premiums as advance payment on premium contribution of the LGU.

We recommended that:

1. The implementing offices/department for each of the programmed projects monitor the implementation of projects; and

2. Management considers changes/realignments in the budget for projects which cannot be implemented or have residual balances during the year to other viable developmental projects, subject to the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a checklist of the necessary documents per COA Circular No. 2012-001 to be attached to the disbursement voucher to facilitate the review and enable the person in-charge of pre—audit sufficient knowledge in identifying missing documents per government transaction and require the submission of the same before a certification is made thereof.

We recommended that the GFPS Council consider changes in the DILG endorsed GPB to other gender-responsive PPAs which may be implemented within the year, provided that the LCE shall inform the concerned DILG Office in writing of the changes made therein.

Enforce full liquidation of cash advances within the reglementary period provided under COA Circular No.97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

For the unpaid penalty totaling P.952 million, the LGU may make representations with the MDFO-DOF and ask for waiver or negotiate with a much lower amount; and 

the Municipal Accountant to book up a receivable from the persons liable for the penalty paid by the LGU.

 Purchase its common-use supplies and equipment thru DBM-PS to avail of the most advantageous price. Strict compliance regarding the conduct of bidding for acquisition of goods and services exceeding P200,000.00 (for 3rd class municipalities) and on the prohibition of splitting of purchase requisitions to avail of bulk discounts.

We recommended that:

The Municipal Mayor effect the full utilization of the LDRRMF in order to strengthen the Municipality’s disaster preparedness.

observations.managementAction: Municipal government submitted the liquidation documents relative to the cash advance totaling P800,000.00.  Further, the Municipal accountant committed not to grant subsequent cash advance for Confidential Fund if previous cash advance were not liquidated.
observations.reason: There were no subsequent cash advances relative to intelligence fund granted to the LCE.

observations.recommendation:
Demand the concerned officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances to effect immediate settlement of cash advances, otherwise, the necessary sanction for its settlement as provided for in paragraph 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012 shall be enforced; and

Enforce full liquidation of cash advances within the reglementary period provided under COA Circular No.97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
observations.managementAction: Management adopted control measures and will not grant additional cash advances to those officials and employees who have still outstanding balances of cash advances.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant examine the completeness of the supporting documents to ensure validity, propriety and legality of the transactions and avoid suspension of disbursements in audit.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant stressed that a strong internal control is created relative to the checking of pertinent supporting documents.
observations.reason: There were still cash advances that lacked supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor follow the guidelines on travel under EO No. 298 and the Municipal Accountant to examine the completeness of the supporting documents to avoid suspension/disallowance of disbursements in audit.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to follow the guidelines provided in EO No. 298.
observations.reason: There were still cash advances for travelling expenses  not in accordance with the guidelines on travel under EO No. 298

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant examine the completeness of the supporting documents for every transaction of the municipality before payment shall be done to avoid possible suspensions and/or disallowances in audit and to strictly comply with the requisites as provided in DBM Local Budget Circular No. 103 dated May 15, 2013.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant justified that RATA was granted in full because there was no basis in making deductions for such absences incurred since the provision of the DTR was not yet effectively in place.  She also said that starting January 2016, the use of the Biometric System will solve the issue.
observations.reason: The DTRs of the heads of various offices of LGU –Margosatubig are properly filled-up and submitted on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant examine the completeness of the supporting documents for every transaction of the municipality before payment shall be done to avoid possible suspensions and/or disallowances in audit and to strictly comply with the requisites as provided in DBM Budget Circular No. 2009-3 dated August 18, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant explained that PERA was granted in full because there was no basis in making deductions for such absences incurred because the DTRs together with the approved application for leave were not submitted on time for the preparation of the payroll. She also said that starting January 2016, the use of the Biometric System will solve the issue.
observations.reason: The DTRs of the heads of various offices of LGU –Margosatubig are properly filled-up and submitted on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant examine the completeness of the supporting documents for every transactions of the municipality before payment shall be done to avoid possible suspensions and/or disallowances in audit and to strictly comply with the requisites as provided in DBM-DOH Joint Circular No. 1, s2012 dated November 29, 2012, pursuant to Republic Act No 7305 entitled as “Magna Carta of Public Health Workers”.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant committed that the breakdown of specific Magna Carta items be reflected in the payroll to ensure identification of specific expenditures and benefits.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipality comply with the rules and regulations in DBM Budget Circular No. 10 dated March 29, 1996, as repealed by CSC-DBM Joint Circular No. 1 s.2015 dated November 25, 2015, and Section 5.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, to ensure validity, propriety and legality of the disbursements. In addition, the municipality shall strictly comply with the rules and regulations espoused in Item a, Section 138 Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM) and refrain from disbursing government fund without an appropriation, law or other specific statutory authority.
observations.managementAction: Management assured compliance with the rules and regulations in DBM Budget Circular No. 10 dated March 29, 1996, as repealed by CSC-DBM Joint Circular No. 1 s.2015 dated November 25, 2015, and Section 5.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the annual appropriation for the JHCSC-Margosatubig Extension Campus be included in the appropriation ordinance of LGU-Margosatubig and be enacted by its Sangguniang Bayan.
observations.managementAction: Management emphasized that there is a need to check and to analyze the MOA entered between the LGU and the JHCSC to identify and determine the role of the former especially in the financial affairs and management of the State College so that proper treatment and accounting of the financial transactions could be done.
observations.reason: In CY 2016 Annual appropriation for the JHCSC-Margosatubig Extension Campus was already included in the appropriation of the LGU and was enacted by the SB

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local School Board comply with the rules and regulations as provided in Title IV of RA 7160 to ensure validity, propriety and legality of the disbursements.
observations.managementAction: Management adopted measures to ensure that the budget for Special Education Fund (SEF) will be duly approved by the Local School Board and Sangguniang Bayan.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that appropriation for 20% EDF be optimally utilized, thus, the local chief executive shall ensure compliance with the responsibilities provided for in paragraph 5.0 of DILG and DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1 to achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.  Further, the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Development Council shall properly formulate the annual public investment programs by properly identifying PPAs and monitor the implementation of the projects as incorporated in the Annual Investment Plan.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to comply with the guidelines provided in for in paragraph 5.0 of DILG and DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1 and to properly formulate the AIP and monitor the implementation of the various projects, programs and activities.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that appropriations for MOOE and CO for the Office of the MDRRM be included in the annual budget of the local government to provide sufficient funds for the DRRM programs.
observations.managementAction: LGU created measures to avoid expenditures against LDDRM fund which were not in consisted with the guidelines.
observations.reason: The LGU in its CY 2016 budget, appropriated MOOE to the Office of the MDRRM

observations.recommendation: We recommend that municipal officials involved in the approval and payment of the transactions examine the completeness of the supporting documents to ensure their validity, propriety and legality and avoid suspension of disbursements in audit.
observations.managementAction: Management adopted control measures to ensure that the municipal officials involved in the approval and payment of the transactions should examine the completeness of the supporting documents.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipality comply with the rules and regulations on the contract of services, particularly stipulations made in the Job Orders/ Appointments to comply with the Civil Service Commission Resolution No. 020790 dated June 5, 2002, to ensure validity, propriety and legality of the disbursements.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to comply with the rules and regulations on the contract of services, particularly stipulations made in the Job Orders/ Appointments to comply with the Civil Service Commission Resolution No. 020790.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipality comply with the rules and regulations on the contract of services, particularly stipulations made in the Job Orders/ Appointments to comply with the Civil Service Commission Resolution No. 020790 dated June 5, 2002 and COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, to ensure validity, propriety and legality of the disbursements.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant justified that JO’s wages were already paid on a daily basis, though, DTRs were sometimes submitted late.  But starting January 2016, JOs who failed to submit DTR and Accomplishment Report are not compensated.
observations.reason: In CY 2016, the LGU already complied with the rules and regulations of contract of services.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipal government refrain from disbursing excessive disbursements as defined under Section 5 of COA circular No. 2012-003 to avoid disallowance. Also, the Municipal Accountant examines the completeness of the supporting documents relative to the payment of tires and repair of government vehicle to ensure validity, propriety and legality of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: Management justified that the government vehicle (Toyota HiLux 248) was already significantly depreciated, hence, mores repair were incurred. Also the municipal accountant committed to examine the completeness of the supporting documents and submit the lacking documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the BAC follow the guidelines as provided in Appendix 26 of the Handbook on Philippine Government Procurement Sixth Edition relative to the Pre-Selection Procedure in the Conduct of Limited Source Bidding to avail the most advantageous price in favor of the municipal government.  In addition, warranty security be imposed to ensure that manufacturing defects be corrected by the supplier.
observations.managementAction: Management is in the process of filing an appeal regarding the Notice of Disallowance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the BAC follow the guidelines as provided in Appendix 18 of the Handbook on Philippine Government Procurement Sixth Edition relative to Shopping and Small Value Procurement to avail the most advantageous price in favor of the municipal government.  In addition, Municipal Accountant to examine completeness of supporting documents for every transaction of the municipality before payment shall be done to ensure validity, propriety and legality of the disbursements.
observations.managementAction:
BAC committed to follow the guidelines as provided in Appendix 18 of the Handbook on Philippine Government Procurement Sixth Edition relative to Shopping and Small Value Procurement.

Management is in the process of filing an appeal regarding the Notice of Disallowance.
observations.reason: In CY 2016, there were still transactions that in contrast with the guidelines for small value procurement.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the BAC follow the guidelines as provided in Appendix 18 of the Handbook on Philippine Government Procurement Sixth Edition relative to Shopping and Small Value Procurement to avail the most advantageous price in favor of the municipal government.  In addition, Municipal Accountant to examine completeness of supporting documents for every transaction of the municipality before payment shall be done to ensure validity, propriety and legality of the disbursements.  Lastly, record the purchases of school supplies held for distribution as inventory and the corresponding specific expense account be recorded upon issuance, transfers or other disposal.
observations.managementAction:
BAC committed to follow the guidelines as provided in Appendix 18 of the Handbook on Philippine Government Procurement Sixth Edition relative to Shopping and Small Value Procurement.

Management is in the process of filing an appeal regarding the Notice of Disallowance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that municipal officials involved in the approval and payment of the transactions examine completeness of the supporting documents and that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) strictly comply with the conditions for the use of the Alternative Mode of Procurement provided under Revised IRR of Republic Act No. 9184 in the procurement of supplies and materials, to ensure validity, propriety and legality of the disbursements.
observations.managementAction:
LGU assured that the BAC will create measures to avoid mistake and strictly comply with the conditions for the use of the Alternative Mode of Procurement alternative method of procurement.

Management is in the process of filing an appeal regarding the Notice of Disallowance.
observations.reason: In CY 2016, there was still procurement of fireworks but instead of being purchase under direct contracting the BAC resorted to limited source bidding and there were still deficiencies in the procurement process as discussed extensively in the part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant examine completeness of the supporting documents for every transaction of the municipality before payment shall be done and to strictly comply with the requisites as provided in COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007 and Item No. 2, COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, to ensure validity, propriety and legality of the disbursements on fund transfers to NGOs/POs.
observations.managementAction: Management is in the process of filing an appeal regarding the Notice of Disallowance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that officials and/or employees adhere to the provisions of Section 340 of GAAM when having official travel to promote economy in the usage of the scarce resources of the municipal government.
observations.managementAction: LGU assured to adhere to the provisions of Section 340 of GAAM when having official travel and create measures that will promote economy and efficiency relative to the official travel of the municipal officials.
observations.reason: In CY 2016, management complied strictly with the provisions of Section 340 of GAAM.

observations.recommendation: Management refrain from granting financial assistance to support the activities of the various organizations which are not falling within the mandate of the municipal government.
observations.managementAction: Management justified that the P50,000.00 financial assistance granted to Lawn Tennis Association was in line with the celebration of the Araw ng Margosatubig. Moreover, other claims were believed to be within the scope of government expenditures since it can be considered that the LGU will be benefited.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that municipal officials involved in the approval and payment of the transactions examine the completeness of the supporting documents and refrain from granting claims in excess of the estimated budget/budgetary requirements to ensure validity, propriety and legality of the disbursements. Further, the Municipal Accountant shall prepare an adjusting entry to reclassify the expense from financial assistance to Cash Advance of the respective Sangguniang Bayan Members.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to strictly examine the supporting documents on their cash advances. Municipal Budget Officer also strictly monitors the budgetary requirements.
observations.reason: Municipal accountant reclassified the expenses as cash advances.  Also, refund for the said Cash advances was made but the municipal accountant has not yet communicated such refund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipality comply with the rules and regulations on the employee’s monthly net take home pay, particularly stipulations made in the General Provisions of the General Appropriations Act for FY 2015 that the same shall in no case be lower than Three Thousand Pesos (P3,000.00) to ensure validity, propriety and legality of the disbursements.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant justified that there was no control in the granting of GSIS loans since application and approval of loan is to be made online. But, in order to correct the deficiency, the payroll clerk shall have full coordination with the liaison officer who verifies online loan application to avoid granting of loans to employees with net take home pay of less than P3,000.00.
observations.reason: In CY 2016, the LGU complied with the rules and regulations on the employee’s monthly net take home pay.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the Heads of the various offices of LGU-Margosatubig to prepare their respective PPMPs and submit to the Municipal Budget Office for review and be included in budget proposal for CY 2016 subject to the approval of the local chief executive.  Upon approval, the said documents should be submitted to the BAC Secretariat for consolidation into the proposed Annual Procurement Plan.
observations.managementAction: BAC Secretariat said that in CY 2015 each of the offices prepared the APP instead of the PPMPs.  In 2016, the various offices already prepared the PPMP for consolidation to the APP of the municipality.
observations.reason: In CY 2016, PPMPs of various offices of LGU-Margosatubig were prepared and submitted and integrated in the APP.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Property Officer to issue ARE for all equipment issued for the official use of officials and employees.  Also, the Municipal Accountant to require submission of ARE before payment be made for all non-expendable property and equipment as required under COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to create Inventory and Inspection Team to conduct an inspection and inventory taking of all assets under PPE in the 2nd quarter of 2016 and the property officer to issue ARE for all equipment issued for the official use of officials and employees.
observations.reason: Reiterated in the Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the municipal treasurer to enforce the remedies and measures prescribed under Section 254 and 258 of RA 7160 on delinquent real property taxes. Strengthen collection system through intensified tax campaign to eliminate and/or minimize delinquencies.
observations.managementAction: Municipal government committed to make necessary measures to collect delinquent real property taxes.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer facilitate the immediate remittance of withholding taxes and contributions particularly those pertaining to prior years due to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and Philhealth within the prescribed period to enable these agencies to use the needed funds for its operations in a timely manner and to preclude delay in the processing of members/ employees’ claims and prevent possible forfeiture of the benefits due them to protect their interest.
observations.managementAction: Management see to it that funds withheld for the BIR, GSIS, PAG-IBIG and PhilHealth will be remitted on time.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the BAC adhere to the guidelines set forth in the Revised IRR of RA 9184 when conducting procurement to avoid incurrence of irregular expenditures.  In addition, LGU-Margosatubig should make a demand to the supplier/contractor for the completion of the aquamarine farming project and the delivery of undelivered items.  Further, liquidated damages should be imposed by the LGU-Margosatubig to the supplier/contractor for failure to completely implement the aquamarine farming project and the non-delivery of the procured items based on Section 8 of Annex “E” of the Revised IRR of RA 9184. Lastly, management should initiate the blacklisting of the said supplier/contractor of the aquamarine farming project using the Uniform Guidelines for Blacklisting as provided in Appendix 11 of the Handbook on Philippine Government Procurement Sixth Edition.
observations.managementAction: Management filed an appeal regarding the Notice of Disallowance.
observations.reason: Pending appeal

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant should prepare the required Bank Reconciliation Statement so that the reconciling items be recorded in the books for the fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant updated the Bank Reconciliation Statement monthly in CY 2015 to reflect the correct amount of Cash in Bank and to address immediately the reconciling items.  However, the nature of the difference of P5,856,800.80 was not fully determined.
observations.reason: During exit conference, the Municipal Accountant disclosed that  reconciliation was made and they were able to verify part of the reconciling items and an estimate of three (3) million remained unreconciled.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant should submit the supporting schedules of the Accounts Payable and Other Payable accounts to establish validity of the obligations to the creditors.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant did the necessary undertaking to identify the amounts and maintain the schedule from time to time for the current year and onwards.  However, schedules to support valid claims were not prepared and not submitted.
observations.reason: During exit conference, the Municipal Accountant disclosed that there is an on-going retrieval of records, however, only a part of records on the P4,043,871.39 was retrieved but they will continue to exert more effort to identify the remaining items.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant shall strictly comply with the rules and regulations on travel as provided in E.O 298 and on cash advances as provided in PD 1445.

We also recommend that the officials of the municipality to refrain from charging personal travelling expenses against the government funds.
observations.managementAction: Management adopted control measures to ensure that cash advances shall only be granted to officials who are legally authorized and only for duly authorized specific purpose.

observations.recommendation: Official and some employees of the municipal government who are performing regular functions at the JHCSC-Margosatubig Extension Campus should stop claiming honoraria for their services rendered.
observations.managementAction: Officials and employees of the municipal government did not claim honoraria for their services at JHCSC-Margosatubig Extension Campus.
observations.reason: Management refrained the practice of granting the said honoraria.

observations.managementAction: Management already directed the GAD Focal person to ensure that GAD plan was duly reviewed by the DILG and  copy of which and of the Accomplishment Report  submitted to COA Audit Team.
observations.reason: During exit conference, the GAD Focal person disclosed that the CY 2016 GAD plan was prepared and reviewed by her and the DILG Officer.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant should submit the reports and documents relative to collections and disbursements of government funds on time to conduct a timely verification and audit of the accounts otherwise the sanctions as provided in Section 122 of P.D. 1445 shall be imposed.  In addition, submit justification for the delay in the submission and for not able to submit all the required CY 2014 reports and documents.
observations.managementAction: Management exerted efforts and adopted control measures for the submission of the required documents on time.
observations.reason: Not submitted on time.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the LDDRMF should be accounted properly following the guidelines provided in COA Circular 2012-002, thereby, Due to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds should be recognized and the Trust Liability-DRRM should be recorded under the TF books. 
 
Also, the total amount of unexpended balance of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund for the Quick Response and Mitigation Funds – MOOE totaling P3,053,235.52 should be transferred to the special trust fund in the Trust Fund Books in CY 2015 to support disaster risk reduction and management activities of the LDRRMCs within the next five (5) years pursuant to Section 21 of Republic Act 10121.
observations.managementAction: Management adopted control measures to ensure that LDDRMF shall be accounted properly following the guidelines provided in COA Circular 2012-002.
observations.reason: No transfer of funds was yet effected to the special trust fund in the Trust Fund Books.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant shall record all transactions accurately using the correct expense codes prescribed in the NGAS Manual.  She should also make a thorough review of the work of her subordinates to avoid repetitive and indiscriminate use of wrong expense codes.
 
The Municipal Budget Officer should advise the department heads of offices concerned of the status of their funds.  In the event that there are savings in other items of the same class, a request can be made for augmentation to cover for the necessary expenses prior to the closing of accounts at year end.
observations.managementAction: Management already directed the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to reconcile their respective records monthly.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the agency ensure the procurement of common use supplies shall be made from the Procurement Service, in compliance with RA 9184, Administrative Order No. 17 and DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6 dated August 25, 2011.  This way, the agency is one with the government in its support of transparency, competitiveness, streamlined procurement process, system of accountability, and public monitoring.
observations.managementAction: Management assured to procure   common use supplies of the Municipality from the Procurement Service Depot.
observations.reason: Not all procurement made at PS-DBM.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer/ GSO Advise all end users and accountable officers to prepare Property Return Slips covering the unserviceable property items in their custody and actually return said items. Prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Property, and File an application of the disposal of unserviceable property included in the Inventory and Inspection report, in accordance with COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: Management required the Municipal Treasurer to conduct physical inventory of its fixed assets and inventory of unserviceable properties.
observations.reason: Municipal Treasurer did not prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Property but the LGU conducted the Physical Count for various vehicle only.

observations.recommendation: It is required that the vouchers for monetization of leave credits of Hon. Ricky Golez and Hon. CatalinoTano be immediately submitted for post audit. Likewise, these cash advances should immediately be refunded.    Refrain from the granting of cash advances to elective officials except for their official traveling expenses as allowed by law.
observations.managementAction:
Management required these officials to refund the amount.

Some elective officials have already refunded their cash advance for monetization of leave credits.
observations.reason: It was noted that the management was unable to furnish  the Auditor a copy of the pertinent documents relating to the said refund.
